source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 10.2.2 Managing a tar backup (pp. 181-183)

questions.q: Append the file /etc/passwd to the existing uncompressed backup /backups/home.133.tar.
questions.why: The r key appends files or directories to the end of an archive.

questions.q: What is the difference between the tar keys r and A?
options: r appends files/directories to the archive; A appends the contents of one archive to another
r replaces the archive; A creates it
questions.why: r adds files/dirs to the end of the archive; A appends all files from one archive into another.

questions.q: Can you add files to a COMPRESSED tar backup (e.g. backup.tgz)?
options: No - tar reports 'Cannot update compressed archives'
Yes, but only with the A key
questions.why: Appending is not possible on a compressed archive; tar refuses with 'Cannot update compressed archives'.

questions.q: List the contents of the uncompressed backup backup.tar without extracting it.
questions.why: The t key lists the archive's contents (v adds detail); it works whether or not the archive is compressed.

questions.q: When listing or extracting, do you need to specify the compression algorithm used?
options: No - 'tar tvf' reads a gzip or bzip2 archive just as well; the algorithm only matters when CREATING
Yes, you must always add z or j to read
No, and you also don't need it when creating
questions.why: tar auto-detects compression when reading, so tar tvf == tar tvfj for listing; you only choose the algorithm at creation time.

questions.q: Create the backup file_name.tar of dir/ AND verify its integrity at creation time (W key).
questions.why: The W key verifies the archive as it is written; it cannot be used on compressed archives.

questions.q: How do you verify a COMPRESSED backup, since W does not work on it?
options: Use the d key, e.g. 'tar dfz file_name.tgz'
Use the W key with sudo
You cannot verify compressed archives at all
questions.why: The d key compares an existing archive against the filesystem and works on compressed backups (W does not).
